📧 Email intake first response

Email received → first reply. Coming next.

How metrics are tracked: Each customer-facing transaction records start (submitted) + end (decided) timestamps. We surface the timing data only — no behavioral surveillance. Median / avg / P90 give you the typical, mean, and tail-risk view. Oldest pending flags work that's slipping. By-community breakdown surfaces which book of business needs attention.

📬 Inbox

Loading…
📬
Pick a thread to read
Threads sorted by most recent activity. Color dots show SLA status — green is healthy, yellow/red need attention, gray is closed.

📑 Reports

Drag-drop a Vantaca-format PDF (DRV summary, violation detail, AR aging, work order list). AI extracts the data and Bedrock renders the customer-facing artifact. Boards see your brand, not Vantaca's.

📤
Drop a Vantaca report PDF here
or click to browse — AI auto-detects the report type
⌨️ Manual entry — type the data in directly (skip the AI)
Read the numbers off your Vantaca report and type them in here. Bypasses AI extraction entirely — the renderer gets your data and produces the newsletter PDF immediately. For the message paragraphs and Top 3 bullets, leave them blank and AI generates them from your numbers, or type your own.
METRIC CARDS (4 numbers)
TOP VIOLATION TYPES (one per line — category, percentage)
Format: Category Name | XX (one per line, no % sign needed)
MESSAGE PARAGRAPHS (optional — leave blank for AI to generate)
Paragraphs separated by blank lines. Leave entire box empty to let AI generate from your numbers.
TOP 3 THINGS TO WATCH (optional — leave blank for AI)
One bullet per line. Leave empty to auto-generate from top categories.

Past Conversions

Loading…

📥 Vantaca Imports

Drop any Vantaca export — AR Aging, GL Export, AP Ledger, Bank Recon, Owner Statements. The system classifies + routes automatically. Every customer-facing surface that reads this data shows the as-of date + time so no one mistakes a snapshot for live state. Until Bedrock replaces Vantaca with a more durable back-office solution, this is the bridge that lets trustEd hold the data we need.

📖 How to upload Vantaca exports click to open

New to this? Each Vantaca export feeds a different surface in trustEd. Below is the playbook for each report type, where to find it in Vantaca, and how often it should be uploaded. The system auto-classifies the file you drop — these procedures are for context, not required reading every time.

📊 AR Aging Report
Feeds: homeowner balance display, portal balance card, collections workflow, certified §209 letter generation.
Vantaca path: Reports → Owner Accounts → AR Aging Report (by Owner).
Export as: PDF.
Cadence: Monthly minimum, weekly during heavy collection seasons.
What to drop: One PDF per community, or drop multiple at once.
📒 Transaction History (Owner Ledger)
Feeds: the per-account running ledger, category breakdown (assessment / interest / late fee / attorney / admin), reconciliation against AR Aging.
Vantaca path: Reports → Owner Accounts → Transaction History (TransactionHistoryAssoc export).
Export as: Excel or CSV preferred (cleaner parsing).
Cadence: Ad-hoc, or any time AR Aging shows a discrepancy that needs investigation.
What to drop: Full community ledger, ideally covering 12 months or more.
📚 GL Export (General Ledger)
Feeds: board financial review, monthly close, audit prep, financial statement generation.
Vantaca path: Reports → Financials → General Ledger Export.
Export as: Excel or CSV.
Cadence: Monthly close, plus year-end snapshot for tax/audit.
What to drop: Full-year or YTD export per community.
🧾 AP Ledger (Accounts Payable)
Feeds: vendor activity history, vendor performance benchmarks, 1099 prep, AP aging review.
Vantaca path: Reports → Vendors → AP Ledger / Vendor History.
Export as: Excel or CSV.
Cadence: Monthly with the close packet, plus year-end for 1099s.
What to drop: Per community, or one combined export if your version supports it.
🏦 Bank Reconciliation
Feeds: monthly close confidence check, board packet bank-rec section, audit trail.
Vantaca path: Reports → Banking → Bank Reconciliation Report.
Export as: PDF.
Cadence: Monthly with the close packet.
What to drop: One per bank account per community per month.
📄 Owner Statements
Feeds: homeowner statement archive (so the portal can show prior statements without re-querying Vantaca).
Vantaca path: Reports → Owner Accounts → Owner Statement (batch print to PDF).
Export as: PDF batch.
Cadence: Monthly with the close packet.
What to drop: A single batched PDF per community is fine; the system splits per account.
📈 Budget vs Actual
Feeds: board financial review, variance analysis, year-end recap narrative for annual reports.
Vantaca path: Reports → Financials → Budget vs. Actual.
Export as: Excel or PDF.
Cadence: Monthly with the close packet.
What to drop: Per community, YTD through close period.
If a file lands in Needs Review
Sometimes the classifier can't tell what community a file belongs to (no community name in header, multi-community export, etc.). Scroll down to the Recent Imports list, find the row with status ⚠ Needs Review, click it, and pick the right community + report type from the dropdowns. Save. The system then routes it normally. No file is ever silently lost; it sits in Needs Review until someone routes it.
Mirror Freshness panel below
The dashboard below this drop zone shows per-community × per-report-type freshness. Green is current, amber is over 7 days old, red is over 14 days old. Use it to spot which community is overdue for an export pull from Vantaca.

Drop a Vantaca Export

PDF, Excel, or CSV. Drop multiple files at once — each gets classified separately. The system identifies community + report type + as-of date from the file itself. Low-confidence files land in a Needs Review queue where you pick the right routing in one click.

📄
Click or drop one or more Vantaca exports
PDF · Excel · CSV — drop a whole month at once

Mirror Freshness

Per-community × per-report-type snapshot of when each data type was last imported. Older than 7 days = amber. Older than 14 = red. Click a row to see the import history for that community + type.

🧾 Bank Reconciliation

Three-way rec: bank statement + check register + GL. The matcher pairs check#s, surfaces outstanding checks + deposits in transit + bank-only items, and computes the reconciled balance. Output is a cheat sheet — paste the numbers into Vantaca's rec module instead of doing the analytical work twice.

Loading…

New Bank Reconciliation

No account showing? Add one now
New Bank Account

📚 Books — General Ledger

Bedrock's own GL. Double-entry posting, period management, trial balance. Pilots: August Meadows (greenfield, June 1) + Quail Ridge (migration). Vantaca CoA structure used as roadmap so future migration is mechanical. By end of Vantaca contract (~18 months), all communities run here.

📚
Pick a community to view its books
August Meadows and Quail Ridge are the Phase 1 pilots.
Pick a date.
Pick a period.
Pick a period.
Pick a period end.

💰 Annual Budget

Drag-drop your Vantaca projected budget PDF (or any HOA budget PDF). System extracts line items, attempts to match each to an account in the chart of accounts by Vantaca account number, and shows a preview before saving. Unmatched accounts surface for manual mapping.

📒 Chart of Accounts

Standard HOA structure. Vantaca account numbers map 1:1 for seamless migration. Summary accounts (headers) are not postable; only detail accounts accept journal entries.

📅 Accounting Periods

Monthly periods. Closed periods reject new postings. Reopen requires a written reason for audit trail.

💵 Accounts Payable

Drop vendor invoice PDFs and trustEd extracts the data, matches the community + vendor, auto-codes the GL account based on vendor history, and posts the journal entry automatically. Invoices land in the Awaiting Approval queue — one click to approve and they're ready for payment.

🗳️ RFP Bid Comparison

Upload 8-10 bids → structured extraction reads scope + pricing + insurance per bid → eliminate vendors with operator-signed reasons → finalize top 3 for board. Every cut is recorded so transparency is shipped with the recommendation.

📅 Annual Meeting Schedule

Portfolio view of every community's annual meeting lifecycle. Sort by what's coming up next, jump into any cycle in one click.

Today:
Sort:
Community
Last year
Start to plan
Send Call by
Nominations close
Annual meeting
Loading…
How dates are computed
Working back from the annual meeting date: Nominations close = T−24 · Send by = close − 21 (or close − 14 if electronic-only) · Start to plan = send-by − 14. Texas Property Code §209.0056 requires notice 10-60 days before the meeting.

📋 Call for Nominations

Run the annual nomination cycle: branded call-for-nominations letter (printed + mailed), a public form for homeowners to self-nominate or nominate a neighbor, and a managed slate going into the annual meeting.

New nomination cycle

Most fields are saved with the cycle and printed on the letter. Community is the one selected in the header.

📂 Working from saved draft . 💾 Save checkpoints your work; ✓ Finalize cycle publishes it and clears this draft.
Annual meeting date
Meeting time (optional)
Meeting location
📥 How homeowners can submit nominations
⚖️ Floor-nominations notice
Per the community's governing documents, nominations from the floor are:
Custom wording (optional — overrides the canned notice; use this to document a board's acknowledged decision to deviate from governing documents)
📅 Annual meeting timeline
Texas Property Code §209.0056 — notice must be sent 10-60 days before the meeting. Bedrock targets T−17 to mail and T−24 to close nominations (7-day buffers). Send-by works back from close: 14 days for electronic, 21 days for mail-back.
Nominations close (leave blank to auto-set T−24)
Close time (so no midnight drop-offs)
Seats open
Term (yrs) (usu. 3)
Letter is dated the day you generate it — that's the "nominations open" date. Public form auto-opens then and closes on the date above.
Current Board (auto-pulled from Roster)
✏️ Edit roster →
Pick a community to load the roster.
Letter context (optional — extra paragraph in the body)
👁️ Standard wording (used when blank)
"Any homeowner is welcome to run for the Board or nominate a candidate — your community's governing documents set the specific eligibility rules, and we'll confirm them before the ballot is finalized."
What's expected of a Board member (optional override)
👁️ Standard wording (used when blank)
"Board members serve a three-year term. The role is volunteer. The board approves the annual budget, oversees the management agent, and represents the homeowners. Meeting cadence varies by community — some boards meet monthly, some quarterly, some only as needed. If you would like to serve, please submit your nomination."
Appears as an italicized sidebar in the letter.
On-site drop-off (optional)
👁️ What's always included in every letter
  • Community logo + Bedrock return address (Sugar Land)
  • Meeting date, time, location + seats open + close date in a hero block
  • Oversized QR code linking to the public nomination form
  • Three submission paths: online (QR), email info@bedrocktx.com, mail to Sugar Land office
  • Standard "proxy and absentee ballot will follow" line
  • Italicized "what's expected of a Board member" sidebar (using your override or the default)
  • Current Board of Directors table (if you entered the board above)
  • Closing signed by Bedrock on behalf of the community's Board
Reference: prior year letter (optional)
Stored with the cycle so next year's cycle can pull it up — does not change what gets printed.
Public form slug (optional)
Homeowners will submit at app.bedrocktxai.com/nominate/<slug> — works on any device, can be hyperlinked from email.

🗳️ Annual Meeting Notice

Phase 2 of the cycle. Pick a cycle whose nominations have closed, confirm the slate, configure voting methods, and generate the 4-page Notice + Ballot + Candidate Statements PDF that goes to every homeowner.

Pick a cycle

—
—

Meeting & voting config

Edit the meeting time, voting cutoff, and electronic-voting toggle here. These print on the Notice + Ballot the way you configure them below.

Voting methods on the Notice

Smart defaults applied automatically based on the config above. The Notice generates with mail + email + in-person enabled, plus online if electronic-voting is on, plus drop-off if configured.

Slate

Candidate order:
—

To add or remove candidates from the slate, go to Call for Nominations → open the cycle → click ✓ Put on ballot on the nomination row.

Next step: push to bedrock-vote

Sends voters + the locked slate to bedrock-vote, where QR-coded mailing labels get generated. Do this BEFORE printing the envelopes — the labels are what go on them.

Recently generated notices

Pick a cycle to see recently generated notices.
Two versions, same notice:
  • 📬 Mailing version — Pages 1-3 only. Drops the candidate biographies to shorten the mailed packet (boards pay per page mailed). The voting instructions point homeowners at the online ballot + community website for bios.
  • 🌐 Website version — All pages including Candidate Statements (bios + photos). Posted on the community website so anyone can read who's running before they vote.
Pages in order:
  • Page 1 — Meeting notice + agenda
  • Page 2 — Voting instructions (methods + bios callout + TX §209.00592)
  • Page 3 — Proxy / Absentee Ballot (3 options, candidate checkboxes, write-in line, owner info, signature)
  • Page 4+ — Candidate Statements (website version only)

🏠 Homes & Owners

Permanent record of every home in every Bedrock community — current owner, current resident, ownership and residency history, owner-occupancy rollups. Upload a Vantaca export to sync changes; review every diff before anything writes to the spine.

Owner-occupancy rollup

Loading…
⚙️ Advanced — Vantaca diff upload (legacy)

Superseded by Roster Import (below). Use this only when you need to preview a raw Vantaca export's diff against the spine WITHOUT verifying rows. For onboarding a community or re-syncing after a Vantaca update, use Roster Import instead — it stamps every row as operator-verified so Vantaca can't silently overwrite your work.

📤 Upload Vantaca export

CSV or Excel. Auto-detects columns. Preview every change before anything saves.

Community
📥
Drag & drop CSV / Excel here
or click to pick a file

📞 Bulk contact methods import

3-tab xlsx with sheets Address / Email / Phone, joined by Vantaca Account ID. Populates the per-email + per-phone notification grid in each homeowner's profile. Inconsistencies are flagged for review, not overwritten.

📥
Drag or click to pick xlsx

🗂️ Roster Import — clean once, verify forever

Download a CSV pre-filled with this community's data → clean in Excel (fix missing zips, NULL out redundant mailings, correct typos) → re-upload → strict validator + preview → apply. Apply stamps every row as operator-verified — future Vantaca syncs surface proposed changes but won't auto-overwrite without your per-row OK. This is the canonical onboarding flow for any new community Bedrock takes on.

Fix the CSV in Excel: required = street_address, city, state, zip, full_name. State = 2 letters. ZIP = 5 digits (or ZIP+4).
Mailing block (4 columns): mailing_street, mailing_city, mailing_state, mailing_zip — fill ALL four for off-property mailings (absentee owners). For owner-occupied homes you can leave all four blank — the system will auto-populate them with the property address on apply (cleaner downstream than the old implicit-blank-means-same convention). Partial fills are still flagged as errors.
Don't change vantaca_account_id (it's the match key).
📤
Drag & drop cleaned CSV/Excel, or click to pick a file

📬 What's changed since you verified the roster?

Upload a fresh Vantaca Mailing Addresses Export (.xlsx) for this community. The system compares it against your verified roster and shows you the real changes (transfers, mailing-address updates, parse bugs) while filtering out formatting noise (e.g., "John & Mary Smith" vs "John Smith & Mary Smith" — same people). Review per-row, approve only the changes you want.

Best Vantaca export to use: Homeowner Contact Information (3 tabs — Address / Email / Phone). The parser auto-detects it and filters the Address tab to Primary Mailing = Yes rows only, eliminating the alt-address noise we hit with the older Mailing Addresses Export. The older export still works as a fallback but may flag false positives when an account has multiple addresses on file.
📥 Drag & drop Vantaca Mailing Addresses Export

💼 Bedrock Office — Client Billing

Generate fixed and activity invoices to HOA clients. Rates come from the active management contract.

⚙️ Contract Defaults (Bedrock standard rate sheet + legal body — applied when a new community contract is created)
Default per-lot monthly fee
Default term (months)
Contract body (legal boilerplate)

Paste the management-agreement text here once. Use merge tokens for the variable bits: {{community_legal_entity}}, {{community_address}}, {{effective_date}}, {{term_summary}}, {{monthly_fee}}, {{annual_fee}}, {{bedrock_legal_name}}, {{bedrock_address}}. Insert {{rate_sheet}} and {{signature_block}} where you want them — if omitted, both auto-append at the end.

Default fixed items (JSON)
Default reimbursables (JSON)
Default owner charges (JSON)

Choose a client
Select a community to view its contract, generate invoices, or edit drafts.

📤 Upload / Update Contract

Drop a Bedrock-Association management contract PDF. The system extracts the fee schedule, you review & correct, then save populates the contract tables. The previous active contract is automatically superseded.

The system reads it and extracts the fee schedule

📜 Active Contract

📃 Management Agreement

Per-lot math is internal — only the resulting monthly fee prints on the agreement. Boilerplate legal text + rate sheet defaults live in Contract Defaults at the top of this tab.

Stage line items that won't come from the Vantaca file (one-off events, special services, write-in postage, etc.). They get appended when you click Generate. To add a NEW one-off description (e.g., "Back to School Bash - Admin Fee"), pick the closest category and edit the description on the invoice detail view after generating.

🏢 Vendors — Master & Invoice Intake

Drop any vendor invoice PDF — Bedrock parses, deduplicates the vendor, files the invoice, and starts building your audit-ready vendor master. Layer 3 in the making.

📥 Drop a Vendor Invoice

PDF only. The system extracts vendor identity, invoice number, dates (including service period for accrual-aware matching), and line items. New vendors are added to the master automatically with fuzzy dedup.

📄
Click or drop one or more vendor invoice PDFs
Vantaca AP screen, AppFolio bill, scanned paper — drop a whole month at once if you want

🗂️ Vendor Master

Loading…

🛡️ Insurance Renewal Analysis

Year-over-year analysis tool for HOA insurance renewals. Drop the carrier's quote, Bedrock extracts the policy structure, compares to last year, and benchmarks the premium % change against Bedrock's portfolio. Analyst, not broker — the licensed agent of record binds coverage; this surfaces what the board should ask about before they approve.

Loading…

New Renewal Analysis

📚 Help — Operational Training Layer

Ask anything. Answers come from your loaded knowledge base (vendor admin guides, SOPs, agreements) in Bedrock voice with source citations. Built so new hires are productive in week 1, not month 6.

🔍 Ask a question

Tip: Ctrl/Cmd + Enter to submit
📎 Sources

▼ 📦 Loaded Knowledge Base

Drop a PDF (vendor admin guide, user guide, agreement, internal SOP, etc.). The system extracts the text page-by-page and indexes it for semantic search. Takes 30-90 seconds depending on length.

⚖️ Legal reference metadata
Reference only — confirm with counsel for legal advice. Statutes themselves are public; the compilation/commentary belongs to the publisher.
📄

Click or drop the PDF here

Loading…

📖 Knowledge Base — laws, regs & policy

Reference articles Claire and askEd retrieve when answering homeowners and boards. Drop in a LegalClarity piece, RMWBH alert, statute text, CAI publication — it gets chunked, embedded, and surfaced when a relevant question comes up. The smarter this gets, the smarter Bedrock gets.

Suggested: lien_priority, foreclosure, bankruptcy, homestead, property_tax, stay_362, sec_209, drv, acc_arc, payment_plan, escrow, redemption, fdcpa, fair_housing, open_meetings, board_governance, insurance, reserves
Minimum 200 characters. Paste plain text or markdown. Strip nav, ads, footer boilerplate first.
Loading articles…

🗂️ Documents — Community Library

Drop a PDF, the system figures out what it is. trustEd tracks expirations, flags duplicates, surfaces gaps, and serves docs back when asked in plain English. Single source of truth for community documents.

🧠 askEd coverage

Admin-only diagnostic panel. The scheduled documents_auto_reindex job runs daily at 05:00 Central and drains the unindexed queue automatically; this button is only needed if you want to force an immediate backfill. Re-OCR suspect scans catches docs uploaded before the OCR pipeline existed — flips them back to pending so the queue re-processes them through the scanned-PDF fallback.

Loading coverage…

⚖️ Governing Doc Health

Portfolio overview surfaces every community's Declaration / Bylaws / Rules / Articles state at a glance — counts per category, pending amendments, probably-stale, missing dates, zero-chunk docs. Click a community in the matrix to drill into its detail panel.

Click 🌐 Portfolio overview or pick a community to start.

📜 Pending amendment reviews

The AI flagged these uploads as amendments to an existing doc. Confirm the parent doc + sections so retrieval treats the amendment as current and the original as superseded for those sections.

Filter:

🔗 Connect history to property + homeowner records

Walks every ARC decision and ingested email, matches the address + homeowner name to the property/contact spine. After this runs, the system can answer "show me everything about 123 Forest Lane" — across decisions, emails, future inspections — in one query. Idempotent. Re-run after Vantaca syncs new properties.

🔍 Find a document

📥 Upload documents

No metadata needed — drop the file and trustEd extracts the fields
If locked, every doc in this batch goes to that community regardless of what the system detects in the file.
📄

Click or drop one or more PDFs

~30-60 seconds per document for metadata extraction

📊 Document Coverage Matrix

Each row = a community. Each column = a document category. Cells show status: 🟢 current · 🟡 expiring soon · 🔴 expired · ⚪ missing · ⬜ N/A.

Loading…

⚠️ Unassigned Documents

Couldn't auto-match these to a community — pick one to assign

These uploaded but with community_id = NULL, usually because the doc didn't name the community internally (recorded amendments, generic templates, etc.). Use the community-lock dropdown on future uploads to prevent this.

Loading…

📜 Legacy Library

From the original Lakes of Pine Forest askEd index — not yet categorized

These documents were ingested before the Documents Tracker existed. askEd can still search them, but they have no category, period, or expiration tracking. Click Categorize to promote a doc into the Tracker with full metadata extraction. Already-migrated docs hide from this list automatically.

Loading…

📑 Forms & Applications

Resident-facing form templates — ARC applications, key fob requests, pool access, lease disclosures, and other forms owners need. Upload through the Documents tab; this view is for finding and sending.

Available Forms

To add a new form, upload it through the Documents tab
Loading…

👥 Board Roster

Each community's current Board of Directors. Edit once here and the roster flows into nominations cycles, board packets, and meeting minutes automatically.

Tip: Set each member's term end date so trustEd can automatically flag who's up for re-election when you create a nominations cycle.
No board members on file for this community. Click + Add member to start.
Name Position Term Status Contact

Add board member

🏛️ County Appraisal Records

Drop a FBCAD or HCAD property roll CSV for a community — the system AI-detects the column mapping, matches each row to a property in the spine, and shows you the preview before anything persists. Approved records light up the Community Map's tenure / investor / value / recent-sale layers and surface "per county" ownership for cross-reference against Vantaca. County records are CANONICAL for legal ownership; Vantaca is canonical for the assessment payer.

📊 Coverage by community

Loading…

📥 Upload county roll

🏛️
Drop a FBCAD / HCAD property roll CSV here
or click to pick a file · tip: filter the full county roll to your community's ZIP in Excel before uploading

Review extraction

📋 Ingest history

Loading…

📋 Board Packets

Assemble a Bedrock-branded board packet in ~45 minutes. Each section accepts manual input, PDF upload (system extracts), or auto-fill from trustEd modules. The encoded-Ed voice across cover, exec summary, watch-outs, and action items.

➕ Create New Packet

Loading section templates…

🗂️ Saved packet drafts (pre-creation work in progress)

No saved drafts yet.

📚 Existing Packets

Loading…

🏘️ Community Profile

Operational facts AskEd reads on every question for this community. Set expiration dates on anything that changes seasonally.

Select a community above to view or edit its profile.

📌 Profile fields

Auto-saved when you click Save

🎨 Community brand assets

Upload the Association's logo. It will appear alongside Bedrock's mark on board packets, violation letters, and other customer-facing artifacts. PNG or JPG, ideally 400px+ wide with a transparent background.

No logo
uploaded yet
Brand colors & signoff
Drives the letterhead on community emails. Bedrock-as-invisible-plumbing.

Each community owns its visual identity on customer-facing emails. The community's brand is the hero; Bedrock appears as a small attribution at the bottom.

Live preview · email letterhead
Community Notice
Your Community Name

Hi Jennifer,

This is what the letterhead looks like with the colors above. The email body sits on cream; the band on top reflects the community's identity.

Thanks,
The Your-Community Board
Community management by Bedrock

⚖️ Letter & enforcement settings

Drives every violation letter generated for this community — sender, fees, cure deadlines, payment routing (lockbox), and the master fines toggle.

Paste the exact article/section that grants the Board enforcement power. Renders inline: "…pursuant to Article VII, Section 7.3 of the Association's Declaration…". Leave blank to fall back to the generic CC&R reference.
Admin fees per letter (dollars)
One-time admin fee per mailed letter. Separate from the violation fine itself (set on the per-category fine schedule below or in the Inspect tab → Fine Schedule).
Cure period (days)
Counted from the postmark date (stamped at Mail Queue lock time). Certified §209 should stay at 30 days unless counsel approves otherwise — it aligns with the statutory hearing-request window in Tex. Prop. Code §209.006.
⚖️ §209 letter posture
🟪 Force-mow letter — Declaration citation
These four fields cite the legal authority for the 10-day certified force-mow notice (TX §202.018 + Declaration self-help). Letter dispatch refuses to render without doc number + county + Article — too risky to use placeholders on a certified §202.018 notice that ends up in court. Pull from your Declaration's first page (recording stamp) and the table of contents.
🧱 Builder ARC standards

Objective specifications from the community's design guidelines. The review pipeline checks each new construction submission against these rules FIRST, then references the Design Guidelines PDF for nuance.

The review reads this verbatim. Use it for rules you couldn't structure into the fields above.
🏦 Payment routing (lockbox)
Each association may bank with a different lockbox. The PO Box on the letter is where homeowner checks land — verify with your accountant before changing.

📞 Contacts

Pool company, landscape, security, attorney, board members — anyone you need to reach. Phone is tap-to-dial. Rows turn yellow if the contract expired or it's been over a year since you verified the info. Multiple contacts per vendor are fine — add the account manager, the field supervisor, and the owner as separate rows.

Paste an email body or signature block. The system extracts the contact (vendor, name, role, phone, email, notes) and pre-fills the add-contact form below for you to review and save.

📝 Facts (with expiration tracking)

Add anything ad-hoc — pool hours, swim party rules, holiday office closures. Set an expires date for seasonal items so AskEd flags them as stale.

🔗 Auto-pulled from other data

These come from your vendor contracts table. Click "Override" to replace with manual values (e.g., the contract says one contact but you call a different field rep).

📨 Email Blast

Send a community notice (single community) or a portfolio-wide notice (all communities) to homeowners and current residents. Each recipient receives the email with their community's letterhead and sign-off — even on portfolio-wide blasts. Variables: {{community_name}}, {{community_legal_name}}, {{recipient_first_name}}, {{bedrock_phone}}, {{today_date}}.

Compose

Preview

Sample rendered email(s)

📧 Email Intake

Paste an email thread → trustEd extracts facts, vendors, decisions, and action items → approve to promote into the Community Facts library.

📥
Drag & drop email files here
Accepts .eml, .txt, .pdf, or direct drag from Outlook / Gmail — or
Outlook direct drag captures the body text. If you also want headers + attachments preserved, use Save As → .eml first.
Processing files…
No intakes yet. Click "+ Paste new email" to start capturing email knowledge.

📋 Past ACC decisions (decisions made through trustEd)

Every letter generated through the Review tab is saved here. One-tap re-download of the homeowner letter or the complete packet (letter + application + photos + brochures).

🏗️ Imported ARC history (pre-trustEd letters)

Drop past ARC approval/denial letters or meeting-minute excerpts. trustEd extracts structured fields — review → save. The review engine uses these as informational context (not binding precedent) when reviewing new applications.

📥
Click anywhere here or drop ARC decision letters
Accepts PDF, image (jpg/png), .eml, or .txt

📋 ACC Queue

        
Applications submitted by homeowners. trustEd has already drafted a recommendation for each — your job is to review, approve/modify/deny, and send the response. Speed matters — the homeowner saw the preliminary preview the moment they submitted, and they're waiting on the official decision.

      

      

        

      

    


    

      
      
      
      
      
    


    

      

      

        No applications in this filter. When homeowners submit via the public portal, they'll appear here automatically.

🔐 Portal Users

      

        Manage who can access the board portal + homeowner portal. Invite by email, scope to specific communities (board) or properties (homeowner), generate magic-link invites. Magic-link auth is now live on both portals — click Open ↗ next to either portal to launch the showcase in demo mode. To enable a real community, flip communities.portal_active = TRUE + populate portal_module_config per the homeowner portal showcase memory.

📑 Homeowner Transactions

      

        Upload Vantaca's transaction export for a community. Each homeowner sees their own transactions in the portal with a "Financial activity current as of [date]" disclosure so they know data freshness. Vantaca remains source of truth.
      

    


    
    

      
Upload monthly export

      

        Expected columns: Date, Account Number, Description, Charge, Payment, Balance (header variants tolerated). Re-uploading the same period replaces the previous one — revert if you need to roll back.

Recent batches

        
      

      
Pick a community above to see recent uploads.

    

  








  

    

      
💵 Owner Receivables

      

        Drop a Vantaca AR Aging PDF — the system extracts each property's balance, aging buckets, and status (at-legal, payment plan, etc.). Review the preview, then approve to publish the snapshot. Boards see the latest balance on their property tile labeled as-of [date]. Vantaca remains the live ledger; this is visibility infrastructure.
      

    


    
    

      

        
📅 Cadence status — last AR snapshot per community

        
      

      
Loading…

      

        Cadence: month-end Vantaca AR for each community on the 3rd–5th of the next month. Reminder fires monthly. Ad-hoc mid-month uploads are welcome — both versions coexist and each board package pulls its own period.

              🔗 Category equivalence
              — map non-standard categories ("Sod yard", "Storage Of Unapproved Items") to canonical equivalents so prior-violation history transfers correctly.
            

            
          

          
Click ↻ Refresh aliases to load.

          

            
          

        


        

      

    


    


    
    

      

        
✉️ Letter format — preview each stage with live community config

        

          
          
        

      

      

        

          
          
          
          
        

        

          
          
            ✏️ Edit cure days · fees · sender name · pay-to →
          
        

        

          What you can edit: sender name, sender title, cure days per stage, admin fees per stage, payment URL, pay-to address. Live from the community profile, so a change there shows up here on the next Preview click. Plus you can rewrite the title + opening + closing paragraphs in the Letter edits workspace.

          What's hard-locked (legal defensibility — Texas §209): statutory cure language, hearing-request block, SCRA disclosure, postmark anchor language, §209.006(b)(1) fee disclosure, §209.0064 fine-schedule reference. Editing these requires an engineering + counsel review — talk to me, don't override.
        


        
        

          

            🔍 Enforcement citations
            — populates Authority Statement on every letter. One-time per community.
            
          

          

          

        


        
        

          
          Edit title · opening · closing · signature · footer for each stage, side-by-side with what's rendering today.

              {{property_address}}
              {{category_label}}
              {{phone}}
              {{email}}
              {{owner_salutation}}
              {{sender_name}}
              {{sender_title}}

📋 Month-end violations report

          
        

        
Bedrock-branded printable report. Shows every violation that was OPEN on the chosen date, sorted by street and house number, with the stage they were in on that day (reconstructed from letter history). Designed to drop straight into the board packet.

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
          
Defaults to the last day of last month — the usual board-packet cutoff.

        

        

        

          
          
        

      

    


    
    

      

        
STEP 1📄 Drafts queue — review · approve · reject

        

          
          
          
          
        

      

      

        
No drafts yet. Letters will appear here automatically after each inspection.

      

    


    
    

      

        
STEP 2📬 Mail Queue — approved, ready to print

        
      

      

        
Loading mail queue…

      

    


    
    

      

        
💰 Fine Queue — assessed, awaiting posting to Vantaca

        

          
          
          
          
        

      

      

        
Loading…

      

    


    
    

      

        

          

            
📥 Import historical violations from Vantaca

            
Imported rows: source=vantaca_import · weight 0.5 · unreviewed

          

          
        

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            

              
📥

              
Drop the Vantaca violations export here

              
or click to pick a file — supports PDF (rows extract automatically) or .csv / .xlsx / .xls

            

            
            
PDF: any Vantaca violation history report. CSV/Excel expected columns: Account # / Property Address · Violation Type · Violation Date · Status/Stage (optional) · Resolved Date (optional)

          

          

            
            
          

          
          

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          

            
📎 Bulk attach Vantaca letter PDFs

            
Files all letter PDFs to their violations in one batch

          

          
        

        

          

            Before using this: the violations must already exist in trustEd (via the 📥 Import Vantaca history button). This step ATTACHES the PDF letters to those existing violations.
            


            Filename matching: the system reads each PDF's filename and tries to match it to a property by Vantaca account ID → lot number → street-number prefix. Best results when filenames contain identifiable property info (e.g., VAN12345-letter-courtesy1.pdf or 15711-Crooked-Arrow-2026-03-15.pdf).
          


          

            
            
          


          

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          


          

            
📄📄📄

            
Drag & drop ALL Vantaca letter PDFs here

            
Or click to multi-select (Ctrl+A in the file dialog). Up to 50 PDFs per batch.

            
          


          


          

          

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          

            
⚙ Fine Schedule

            
—

          

          
        

        

          
Loading schedule…

        

      

    


  

  


  
  

    

      

        

          
Add manual violation

          
Neighbor or board report · force-mow walk-up

        

        
      


      

        
        

          ⚠️ Force-mow 10-day certified track — confirm CC&R and §202.018 authority before submitting. This creates the violation only; the certified letter is generated separately via the Drafts queue.
        


        
        

          
          
        


        
        

          
          
          
        


        
        

          
⚠️ Possible duplicate

          

          

            
            
          

        


        
        

          
          
        


        
        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

        


        
        

          
          
        


        
        

          
          
          
          
          

          

            Tap to take photo with rear camera on iPhone/iPad, or pick existing photos from camera roll.
          

        


        
        

        
      

    

  


  
  

    
    
    

  


  
  

    

      

        

          
🔍 Why this draft is at this stage

          
Engine inputs, decision, and what re-evaluate would do.

        

        
      

      

        
Loading trace…

      

    

  


  
  

    

      

        

          
✏️ Fix AI assessment

          
Pick the right violation. Describe what's actually in the photo.

        

        
      


      

        
        

          

            
            
🔍 Click photo to zoom

          

          

            
—

            
—

            

              
⚠ AI's original assessment

              
—

              
—

            

          

        


        
        

          
          
          
Picking a new category auto-fills the description below with starter language for that category. Edit it to match what's specifically in the photo.

        


        
        

          
          
          
This sentence prints inside the letter body. Keep it conversational — what the homeowner sees, not legalese.

        


        
        

          On Save: the violation reclassifies, the observation updates with an audit-trail note ("Reclassified by operator on [date]"), and the letter PDF regenerates with the new category + description. Cure period stays the same — only the category and copy change.
        


        


        
        

          
          
        

      

    

  


  
  

    

      

        

          
—

          
—

🌱 Cures

👥 Team management

    

      Manage who has access to Bedrock and at what role. The role decides what each person can see — admins see everything, staff see the customer-facing surfaces, assistants see a minimum slice.
    


    
    

      
🔑 How to add a team member

      
    
        
  1. Send them this sign-in link: /login.html 
    2. 
        
  2. They sign in with their Microsoft 365 account (whatever email you have on file for them).
    3. 
        
  3. They'll show up in the list below with role staff and active status.
    4. 
        
  4. Come back here and change their role to admin or assistant if needed.
    5. 
        
  5. When you want to revoke their access, toggle them to inactive here. Their Microsoft sign-in still works but Bedrock will refuse them.
    6. 
      

      

        Role guide — admin: full access including internal workpaper · staff: customer-facing surfaces + drafts + send · assistant: read-only / minimum slice (still being defined per module).
      

    


    
    

      

        
Current team 

        
      

      

        

          
            

              Name
              Email
              Role
              Last sign-in
              Active
